brand.name: IAmH3r
brand.vertical: Women's empowerment / personal brand / lifestyle community

summary: IAmH3r is a women's empowerment personal brand and community (DyvaVille, NY) led by a founder, serving women — primarily Black women and women of color — who want to feel seen, affirmed, and connected to a sisterhood as they step into their identity. Acquisition is social-first (X/Twitter, likely Instagram); the likely revenue model is a free community feeding paid membership, coaching, or digital products. Note: the brand site was not resolving at research time, so offer structure, pricing, and tooling remain unconfirmed and should be validated at client kickoff.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Deliver the 'I Am Her' moment immediately — make a new subscriber feel seen, introduce the founder and community, and drive the first affirmation/community action.
campaigns.trigger: New subscriber joins the list (link-in-bio, social CTA, lead-magnet opt-in, or community signup). Send email 1 within seconds.
campaigns.segment: All new contacts; branch later by why-they-joined survey answer (community, coaching, content) if captured.
campaigns.rationale: Every gap is unconfirmed, but for a social-first personal brand the welcome is the highest-engagement, fastest-to-show-value send and the moment to convert a curious follower into a member of the sisterhood before the spark fades.

emails.purpose: Warm, founder-voice welcome that affirms her, sets expectations, and gives one immediate community/identity win.
emails.subject: Welcome to the sisterhood — you're already H3r 👑
emails.preview: Your first affirmation is inside. Two minutes to feel seen.
emails.cta: Read your welcome + claim your first affirmation
keyPoints: Open in the founder's first-person voice — bold, affirming, celebratory of Black womanhood and identity
Tell her she belongs here the moment she joined ('I Am Her' = she already is)
Deliver one quick win now: a signature affirmation, the founder's origin story, or an invite into the community space
Set expectations for what's coming and invite a real reply — a human reads every one
Single clear CTA; no offer or upsell yet — trust is the currency

campaigns.name: Lead-Magnet Nurture
campaigns.objective: Warm a cold social follower from a free download toward the core paid offer (membership/coaching) over a long cycle, building trust and identity connection before any ask.
campaigns.trigger: Opt-in to a lead magnet (suggested: a free affirmation guide, a 'Who Is She?' identity quiz, or a 5-day empowerment challenge).
campaigns.segment: Leads below the 'ready-to-buy' threshold; branch by lead-magnet topic and any quiz/persona answer captured.
campaigns.rationale: Acquisition is social-first with no confirmed trial or booking motion, so a lead magnet is the realistic top-of-funnel capture — and stage-matched nurture is what turns followers into buyers over the weeks-long relationship cycle these communities run on.

emails.purpose: Deliver the asset instantly and frame the deeper problem it speaks to — the feeling of being unseen — so the rest of the sequence has a hook.
emails.subject: Here's your guide — but first, can I be real with you?
emails.preview: The reason you downloaded this isn't really about the guide.
emails.cta: Get your guide + read the truth behind it
keyPoints: Deliver the downloaded asset above the fold — no friction
Name the real problem: feeling unseen, undervalued, or disconnected from her truest self
Position IAmH3r as the sisterhood built for exactly that, in the founder's voice
Set up the journey ahead ('over the next few weeks I'll share...') without selling yet
Soft single CTA to consume the asset; invite a reply with her biggest 'I am...' goal

campaigns.name: Value Newsletter
campaigns.objective: Stay top-of-mind and build the founder's authority and the community bond across the long cycle — 80% value, 20% soft offer — without discounting.
campaigns.trigger: Scheduled recurring cadence (recommend weekly or biweekly).
campaigns.segment: All engaged contacts; suppress active onboarding (Campaign 1) and route the recently dormant into Win-Back instead.
campaigns.rationale: For a personal brand, the founder's voice IS the product — a recurring affirmation + community-stories send is the always-on engine that keeps the list warm, builds the sisterhood, and seeds referrals.

emails.purpose: Establish the repeatable flagship edition: one affirmation/idea, one community story, one soft invitation — the habit-building template.
emails.subject: This week, H3r: the affirmation you didn't know you needed
emails.preview: One truth, one sister's story, one step forward. Let's go.
keyPoints: Lead with one useful idea or affirmation she can use today — the recurring 'This week, H3r' section that builds habit
Spotlight a community win or sister's story for social proof and belonging
Keep it 80% value / 20% soft offer; a single primary CTA (read, reply, or join)
Founder first-person voice throughout — bold, affirming, culturally fluent
Make replying easy; reader replies feed testimonials and re-engagement signals

campaigns.name: Community-to-Paid Conversion
campaigns.objective: Convert a warmed free-community member or nurtured lead into the core paid offer (membership, coaching, or mastermind), handling the 'is this really for me?' objection.
campaigns.trigger: Lead crosses the engagement threshold (consumed nurture, joined free community, replied) OR a free consult/challenge ends; run a behavioral nudge + soft deadline in parallel.
campaigns.segment: Engaged free members and sales-ready leads; branch by depth (highly engaged = direct offer, lurking = re-warm with proof first).
campaigns.rationale: This is the core revenue play — once trust is built, the brand needs a deliberate ask that moves free community members into paid membership/coaching; without it the nurture work never monetizes. Confirm the exact offer and price at kickoff.

emails.purpose: Make the clear, warm invitation to the paid offer — reframe the cost as an investment in herself and dissolve the 'is this for me?' objection with belonging and proof.
emails.subject: You've been H3r all along — ready to make it official?
emails.preview: The doors are open. Here's exactly what's waiting for you inside.
keyPoints: Affirm how far she's come in the community and name that she's ready for the next level
Spell out concretely what the paid offer includes and the transformation/outcome it delivers
Handle the #1 objection head-on: 'is this community actually for me?' — yes, and here's the proof
Member testimonial or founder story showing the before/after of belonging
One low-friction CTA with a gentle reason to act now; offer a real reply to talk it through

campaigns.name: Win-Back / Re-Engagement
campaigns.objective: Reactivate subscribers who went quiet, recover dormant members, and protect deliverability by sunsetting non-responders.
campaigns.trigger: Inactivity threshold (e.g., 60–90 days with no opens/clicks) or a lapsed/cancelled membership.
campaigns.segment: Dormant subscribers (lean on fresh value); separately, churned members (reference what's changed and what they're missing).
campaigns.rationale: Engagement drop-off after the initial excitement is the classic failure mode for empowerment communities, so a win-back is quick to ship, recovers 5–15% of inactive contacts, and keeps sender reputation healthy.

emails.purpose: The 'we miss you' opener — remind her of the core value and that her seat in the sisterhood is still hers.
emails.subject: Did we lose you, sis? Your seat's still here 💛
emails.preview: It's not the same without you. Here's what you've been missing.
keyPoints: Warm, no-guilt 'we miss you' in the founder's voice — she's still H3r, the door never closed
Remind her of the core value: being seen, affirmed, and part of the community
Tease what's new since she went quiet to give a concrete reason to return
Single low-friction CTA back into the community or latest content
Set up the sequence's later sunset step to protect deliverability if she stays silent
